pubmed_links: [Bhatt et al. (2015) Sulf1 and Sulf2 Differentially Modulate Heparan Sulfate Proteoglycan Sulfation during Postnatal Cerebellum Development](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26448642/); [Packer et al. (2022) Autism, heparan sulfate and potential interventions](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35307405/)
summary: SULF1 is a heparan sulfate 6-O-endosulfatase expressed in the brain; Sulf1 knockout mice show deficits in neurite outgrowth, synaptic plasticity, and cerebellar development, and heparan sulfate dysregulation is increasingly implicated as causative in autism spectrum disorder. (emerging evidence)
